sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, William, presented to the office with a complaint of right knee pain that began a week ago after an injury at a trampoline park.
He reports hearing a snap and experiencing instant pain.
He denies taking any medication other than Tylenol, which provided minimal relief.
He has been elevating the knee and has not used ice or heat.
He is a recreational trampoline park visitor and does not have a regular exercise regimen.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension, for which he is currently taking 20 milligrams of lisinopril most days.
He has diabetes and is taking metformin, 500 milligrams daily.
He has not checked his blood pressure recently and has not measured his blood sugar regularly, but has been doing so about 4-5 times a week.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20 milligrams daily for hypertension
Metformin 500 milligrams daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80 mmHg, not within normal limits.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee.
He has a slight limp and limited range of motion due to pain.
The heart rate is regular and rhythm is normal.
Lungs are clear bilaterally.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain in the right knee, likely sustained from a trampoline park injury.
Problem list: Right knee pain; Hypertension; Diabetes; Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Suspected high blood pressure; Unknown status for past hypertension.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 milligrams daily for pain and swelling in the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20 milligrams daily for hypertension and order an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1C and lipid panel to monitor diabetes management.
Follow-up: The patient will return to the office for follow-up after the physical therapy appointment and to discuss the results of the ordered tests.
